content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob recently got into running, and decided to compare how much they ran on different days.

They each ran for NNN days — on the ithi^{th}ith day, Alice ran AiA_iAi​ meters and Bob ran BiB_iBi​ meters.

On each day,


Alice is unhappy if Bob ran **strictly more than twice** her distance, and happy otherwise.
Similarly, Bob is unhappy if Alice ran **strictly more than twice** his distance, and happy otherwise.
For example, on a given day


If Alice ran 200200200 meters and Bob ran 500500500, Alice would be unhappy but Bob would be happy.
If Alice ran 500500500 meters and Bob ran 240240240, Alice would be happy and Bob would be unhappy.
If Alice ran 300300300 meters and Bob ran 500500500, both Alice and Bob would be happy.
On how many of the NNN days were **both** Alice and Bob happy?


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN — the number of days.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ — the distances run by Alice on the NNN days.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​ — the distances run by Bob on the NNN days.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of days when **both** Alice and Bob were happy.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains an integer N, the number of days. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN – Alice's distances. The third line contains N space‑separated integers B1 … BN – Bob's distances.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the number of days on which both Alice and Bob are happy.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: only day 2 satisfies max<=2*min, so answer 1. Test case 2: days 2,3,4 satisfy, answer 3. Test case 3: no day satisfies, answer 0. Test case 4: all five days satisfy, answer 5.

solution.approach: For each day i we check the two happiness conditions. Alice is happy if Bi <= 2*Ai, Bob is happy if Ai <= 2*Bi. Both are happy exactly when max(Ai, Bi) <= 2*min(Ai, Bi). Iterate over all N days, count the days satisfying the condition, and output the count. The algorithm is O(N) per test case and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) – each distance is examined once.
